name: Salim (Servant of Amir)
title: Servant from Basra
shortDescription: The loyal servant of Amir ibn Muslim al-Abdi who accompanied his master from Basra to Karbala and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Salim was the loyal servant of Amir ibn Muslim al-Abdi. Unlike many people who were slaves to the world, he was a freethinking and pious servant.

When his master Amir decided to go from Basra to Mecca to assist Imam Hussain (AS), Salim immediately joined him. Their relationship went beyond that of master and slave; it was a brotherly bond on the path of truth.

Salim was his master's helper and supporter throughout the entire journey. On the day of Ashura, with the start of the enemy army's collective attack, Salim also drew his sword shoulder to shoulder with Amir ibn Muslim.

Having learned bravery from the true Shias, he fought valiantly and, after repelling many attacks, drank the cup of martyrdom in that very first attack alongside his master Amir, becoming a freeman in Paradise.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عامِرِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ وَمَوْلاهُ سالِمٍ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Amir ibn Muslim and his servant Salim.
